relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The Twelve receive power and authority for healing and release.
They are sent to proclaim the Divine reign and heal.
The mission travels without excess support.
Hospitality becomes their temporary dwelling.
Rejected witness is released as dust.
They move village to village with good news and healing.
Herod hears and becomes perplexed.
People interpret Jesus through John, Elijah, and prophets.
Herod’s question becomes: who is this?
The apostles return and Jesus withdraws them toward Bethsaida.
The crowds follow and are received with teaching and healing.
The Twelve see scarcity in the deserted place.
Jesus gives the crowd’s hunger back to the disciples as calling.
The multitude is ordered into groups.
They make space for the feeding.
Blessed and broken bread becomes enough.
All are filled and twelve baskets remain.
Prayer opens the identity question.
The crowds answer through prophetic categories.
Peter names him Messiah of the Divine.
The confession is held in silence.
The Son of Humanity must suffer, be rejected, die, and rise.
Following becomes daily cross-bearing.
Life clutched is lost; life surrendered is saved.
Gaining the world cannot compensate for losing oneself.
The Son of Humanity comes in glory.
Some will see the reign before tasting death.
Jesus takes the three up the mountain to pray.
Prayer transfigures face and clothing into radiance.
Moses and Elijah speak with him.
They speak of his exodus to be fulfilled in Jerusalem.
Sleep gives way to awakened seeing of glory.
Peter tries to preserve the moment in tents.
The cloud overshadows and holy fear rises.
The voice says: listen to the Chosen Son.
Jesus remains alone, and the vision is kept silent.
Mountain glory descends into crowd need.
A father cries for his only child.
The child is seized by a troubling force.
The disciples could not release him.
Jesus names untrust and calls the child near.
Release, healing, and return to the father happen together.
All marvel at divine majesty.
Jesus tells them to place the handing-over word in their ears.
The word remains hidden and they fear asking.
Greatness reasoning enters the disciples.
Jesus answers heart-reasoning with a child.
Receiving the child receives Jesus and the Sender.
John worries about an outsider acting in the name.
Jesus refuses possessive control of the work.
The road turns: he sets his face toward Jerusalem.
Messengers go before him into Samaritan space.
He is rejected because his face is set toward Jerusalem.
James and John want fire in response to rejection.
Jesus rebukes retaliatory zeal.
They simply go to another village.
Someone offers to follow anywhere.
The Son of Humanity walks without settled place.
Another is called but wants first duties first.
The call is to proclaim the Divine reign now.
Another wants farewell before following.
The plow-path asks for forward-facing commitment.
